Z1.conclusion: EMN has negative-slope linear mechanism (slope -1/e^e at depth 2). DEML incompleteness template does NOT prove EMN incomplete.

Z2.mechanism: Uses exp(-large)~=0 to approximate ln(x), then emn(ln(x),1)=-x exactly
Z2.domain: Real x > 0 only (domain errors for x < 0)
Z2.conclusion: EMN approximates neg(x) with error ~1e-7 to ~1e-5 for x in [0.1,10]. Uses complex intermediate values. NOT an exact neg(x) construction. EMN completeness OPEN but much more capable than DEML.

Z3.conclusion: EAL is INCOMPLETE. All linear mechanisms have positive slope. No subtraction available to produce neg(x).

Z4.conclusion: EXL is INCOMPLETE. Cannot build exp(+x) because e is not constructible from EXL({1}). All constant subtrees collapse to 0.

Z5.question: Can EDL approximate add to arbitrary precision?
Z5.answer: EDL cannot exactly add, and approximation does not converge to 0 MSE for x+1
Z5.conclusion: EDL is INCOMPLETE for addition. Not dense in functions requiring addition. MSE stalls above 0.1 at N<=11.

Z6.conclusion: POW is INCOMPLETE over {1,x}. Identity x is 1-node native (remarkable). exp(x) requires e as leaf. POW with leaf e would be complete.

Z7.conclusion: LEX is INCOMPLETE. Identity x = lex(x,0) native but 0 not constructible from {1}. Constant sequence converges toward 0 but never reaches it.

Z8.conjecture: Operator G over {1,x} is incomplete if (A) all linear slopes same sign, or (B) constant e not constructible from G({1})
Z8.emn_status: EXCEPTION — has negative slope AND approximates neg(x) well. Completeness open.

operators_classified.EMN: OPEN (negative slopes, near-exact neg(x) at N=8)
operators_classified.POW: INCOMPLETE (e not constructible, but identity native)

Z9.open_case: EMN — has negative slopes but exact completeness unresolved
